seeded_async_dp_vs_dyna_20state_chain.algorithm: predecessor-aware prioritized sweeping (Sutton & Barto 2nd ed., Fig 8.4)
seeded_async_dp_vs_dyna_20state_chain.note: Predecessor cascade: after discovering reward at state 19, prioritized sweeping propagates values backward through all 19 predecessors in one planning phase. Random Dyna wastes backups on uninformative pairs. 8/10 wins shows consistent advantage.

seeded_dyna_vs_real_one_state.note: 1-state bandit — non-trivial because model compresses R(s,a) but not a meaningful planning test

open_boundaries: Function approximation in the model is linear; nonlinear model-based planning absent
Off-policy accounting for imagined transitions missing
No stochastic environment with confirmed async DP advantage (stochastic model degrades planning quality)
Multi-step and trajectory-level planning (Dyna-2, TreeQN) not implemented
note: Step 7 (2026-05-21): Both Dyna planning and async DP (prioritized sweeping) proven. (1) Dyna vs real-only on 6-state chain: 41.7% more cumulative reward, 132-step convergence speedup, 8/10 wins. (2) Predecessor-aware prioritized sweeping (the paper's actual Step 7 algorithm) vs random Dyna on 20-state chain: 8/10 wins, 0.7368 vs 0.7302 mean final reward. Predecessor cascade efficiently propagates newly-discovered rewards backward through the state space. Remaining open: nonlinear world model, off-policy planning correction, stochastic environments.
